metadata.title: My Flow Check | 3-Day Bladder Diary Tracker
metadata.description: Track when you drink, when you pee, and the patterns behind it in 3 days. A simple bladder diary tool to help you and your health professional.
metadata.ogImageAlt: My Flow Check, a 3-day bladder diary tracker

landing.sessionInProgress: You have a tracking session in progress.
landing.resetWarning: This will delete all your current entries. Make sure you have exported your data first.
landing.heroTitle: Track your drinks and bathroom habits in 3 days
landing.heroSubtitle: Track when you drink, when you pee, and see useful patterns. All in 3 days.
landing.pwaPromptAndroid: Want quick access? You can add My Flow Check to your home screen. No app store needed. Or just keep using it in your browser.
landing.pwaPromptFooter: Or just keep using it in your browser.
landing.privacyBody: Your data never leaves your phone. No account needed, no sign-up, nothing stored on our servers. When you're done, you choose what to share with your health professional.
landing.disclaimer: This app is intended for personal tracking and educational purposes only. It does not provide medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Always consult a qualified health professional regarding any medical condition or health concerns.

onboarding.ageSubtitle: This is useful information for your health professional.
onboarding.unitSubtitle: Choose the unit you're most comfortable with. This will be used throughout the app.
onboarding.dateTitle: When do you want to start?
onboarding.dateSubtitle: Pick a morning you can record your first pee. You will track for 3 days in a row.

timeline.startByWakeUp: Start by adding your wake-up time
timeline.addFirstPee: Add your first pee to start Day {dayNumber}
timeline.overnightQuestion: Did you pee or drink anything overnight?
timeline.useInsertButton: Use the + button to log overnight events, or continue to the next day
timeline.addOvernightFirst: Add all overnight pees and drinks before marking wake-up
timeline.addBeforeBedtime: Add all pees and drinks before marking bedtime
timeline.overnightNocturiaPrompt: Did you get up to pee during the night?
timeline.overnightNocturiaHint: Log overnight pees here so they count. Don't change your bedtime.
timeline.clearAllWarning: This will clear all data from Day 1, Day 2, and Day 3. This can't be undone.
timeline.addFirstEvent: Add your first event of the day

logVoid.doubleVoidDescription: Peed again right after to fully empty
logVoid.secondPeeAmount: How much was the second pee?
logVoid.optionalTapHint: Optional. Tap to select, tap again to clear
logVoid.cupHelpIntro: No measuring cup is fine. Pick the closest size, or pee into any container you can roughly measure.
logVoid.wokeBySubtitle: Helps your therapist tell bladder issues apart from sleep issues.

logLeak.optionalTapHint: optional. Tap to select, tap again to clear

bedtime.whenGoToBed: When did you go to bed?
bedtime.afterWakeUp: Bedtime must be after your wake-up at {time}
bedtime.afterLastEvent: Bedtime must be after your last event at {time}. Overnight events go on the next day.

wakeTime.afterBedtime: Wake-up must be after last night's bedtime at {time}
wakeTime.beforeBedtime: Wake-up must be before your bedtime at {time}

validation.beforePrevBedtime: This time is before Day {dayNumber}'s bedtime ({time}). Pick a later time.
validation.beforeWakeUp: This time is before wake-up ({time}). Daytime events must be after you woke up.
validation.afterWakeUp: This time is after wake-up ({time}). Overnight events must be before you woke up.
validation.afterBedtime: This time is after bedtime ({time}). Go to the night view for overnight events.
validation.duplicateMinute: Another entry is already logged at this minute. Pick a different time.

0.description: Went just in case or social reasons

lifting.label: Lifting
lifting.description: It happened when I lifted something

toilet_way.label: On the way
toilet_way.description: I couldn't quite make it to the toilet

not_sure.label: Not sure
not_sure.description: I'm not sure what caused it

summary.diaryLockedDescription: Complete your 3-day tracking period to unlock your diary and export your data.
summary.reviewDataSuffix: . You can still generate your report, but for the most accurate results consider going back to add any missing entries.
summary.noDrinkEntries: Day {day} has no drink entries - your report will still work but fluid intake data will be missing
summary.ipcDescription: Better data leads to better care. Share these results with your health professional at your next appointment.
summary.heroTitle: You did it. Three days, all noted.
summary.heroSubtitle: Here is what your body told you.
summary.storyBody: Think of your body like a sink. Water goes in, water comes out. Below is the rhythm of your three days. The little drops below show pees. The cups above show drinks.
summary.observationsTitle: What we noticed in your notes
summary.obsCaffeine: Your morning coffee or tea usually nudged you toward the bathroom within an hour or two.
summary.obsEveningFluids: Most of your drinks happened in the late afternoon or evening.
summary.obsMorningFluids: You did most of your drinking in the morning.
summary.obsOneNightWaking: On the night between Day {day} and the next, you got up once. That can happen, especially after fluids in the evening.
summary.obsConsistent: Your bathroom rhythm stayed steady across all three days. That kind of pattern is exactly what helps your healthcare team.
summary.forTeamBody: Your healthcare team reads three days like a short story. They can spot rhythms and habits that are hard to notice from inside. Sending them this report before your visit gives them a head start, so the time you spend together can be about you, not paperwork.
summary.yoursFirst: Read it first, then share your report with your healthcare professional.
summary.privacyNote: Your notes stay on this device. You choose what to share, with whom, and when.
summary.comingBackBody: Your team may suggest another diary in a few weeks or months. Comparing two pictures side by side is how progress becomes visible.
summary.notAlone: Many people find that just paying attention to their habits, the way you did these three days, already starts to change something.

export.noDataYet: Start adding entries to enable export

nextStep.day1StartHint: Tap "Add wake-up time" below when you wake up tomorrow.
nextStep.morningHint: Tap "Add wake-up time" below when you're ready.
nextStep.nightTitle: Log any pees you had during the night
nextStep.nightHint: Use the plus button to add them. Then tap "Add wake-up time" when you wake up.
nextStep.firstEventTitle: Log your first pee or drink
nextStep.firstEventHint: Use the plus button below each time you pee or drink something.
nextStep.keepLoggingTitle: Keep logging as the day goes on
nextStep.keepLoggingHint: Use the plus button each time you pee, drink, or have a leak.
nextStep.bedtimeHint: Before you sleep, tap "Go to bed" below so we know your day is over.
nextStep.staleNudgeHint: Tap the plus button below to log anything you've had since.

milestones.firstEventSubtitle: Your data is saved on this device. Come back anytime
milestones.day1CompleteSubtitle: Great job. 2 more days to go
milestones.day2CompleteSubtitle: You're over halfway. Keep it up!
milestones.day3CompleteSubtitle: Tap View Results to see your diary

day1Celebration.title: Day 1 done!
day1Celebration.evidence: You've captured {count} data points your clinician can use.
day1Celebration.subtitle: Two more days to complete your diary.

day1Celebration.reminderPrompt: Don't forget tomorrow. Pick how we remind you:

day1Celebration.privacyNote: Nothing leaves your device. We don't store your email or phone number.
day1Celebration.skip: No reminder. I'll come back on my own
day1Celebration.icsDescription: Continue logging today. You already captured {count} things on Day 1.
day1Celebration.icsAlarm: Time to log your bladder diary
day1Celebration.shareText: Tomorrow is Day 2 of 3. You already logged {count} things on Day 1.
day1Celebration.helperShareTitle: Remind me about my bladder diary
day1Celebration.helperShareText: Hi! Can you remind me at {time} tomorrow to log my bladder diary? Day 1 is done ({count} entries), 2 days to go. Thanks!

day2Reminder.title: One more morning. Want a reminder?
day2Reminder.subtitle: We'll suggest around {time}. Pick how:

toasts.peeDuplicate: A pee is already logged at that time
toasts.drinkDuplicate: A drink is already logged at that time
toasts.leakDuplicate: A leak is already logged at that time

notifications.morningBody: Don't forget to add your wake-up time and first pee of the day.
notifications.afternoonBody: Have you been adding your drinks and pees? It only takes a second.
notifications.eveningBody: When you're ready to sleep, tap "Go to bed" to wrap up your day.
notifications.completeBody: Your 3-day flow check is complete. Open the app to see your summary.

ipcModal.title: Integrated Pelvic Care
ipcModal.description: At IPC, we believe better data leads to better care. We specialise in male pelvic health and are committed to raising the standard of care in this space. Our tools are designed for everyone, because good health tracking should be accessible to all.
ipcModal.coursesNote: Courses and resources for health professionals

help.title: Help & FAQ
help.description: Frequently asked questions about My Flow Check. Learn how to track drinks, bathroom visits, and understand your 3-day bladder diary.
help.a1: It's a simple 3-day tool to track when you drink, when you pee, and when you go to bed. This helps your health professional spot patterns and recommend the best next steps for you.
help.q2: How do I add a pee?
help.a2: Tap the + button at the bottom of the screen, then tap "Pee". Use the slider to enter how much, rate how strong the urge was, and tap Save.
help.q3: How do I measure how much?
help.a3: Your health professional may provide a measuring jug. Pee into the jug, read the volume in millilitres, then enter it in the app. For reference: a small glass is about 150 mL, a standard glass is 250 mL.
help.q4: What do the urge levels mean?
help.a4: Not at all = you went just in case. A little = slight feeling. Normal = comfortable need. Quite a bit = hard to hold. Couldn't wait = urgent, may have leaked.
help.q5: Why should I add my bedtime?
help.a5: Adding your bedtime helps your health professional tell the difference between daytime and nighttime pees. Waking up at night to pee may need a different approach.
help.a6: Go to the Summary tab to review your data. You can download a PDF or CSV file to share with your health professional at your next appointment.
help.a7: Yes. All your data is stored only on your phone and is never sent to any server. When you export your data, only you decide who to share it with.
help.q8: Can I edit or delete an entry?
help.a8: Yes. On the day timeline, tap the trash icon next to any entry to delete it. You can then re-add it with the correct information.
help.q9: What if I forget to add something?
help.a9: No worries! You can add entries at any time and adjust the time to when it actually happened. Just tap the + button and change the time before saving.
help.stillHaveQuestions: Still have questions? Ask your health professional.
help.suggestionBody: We'd love to hear your ideas for improving the app. Send us an email at

privacy.metaDescription: Privacy Policy for My Flow Check. Your data stays on your phone. We never collect, store, or transmit any personal or health information.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Integrated Pelvic Care Inc. ("IPC", "we", "us", or "our") operates My Flow Check (the "App"). This Privacy Policy explains how we handle information when you use the App.
privacy.shortVersion: The short version: Your data stays on your phone. We do not collect, store, transmit, or have access to any of your personal or health information. Ever.
privacy.section1Text: My Flow Check is designed with a privacy-first approach. The App runs entirely on your device (phone, tablet, or computer) and does not require:
privacy.section1Item2: An email address, phone number, or name
privacy.section2Text: All data you enter into the App, including fluid intake, bathroom visits, bedtimes, wake times, and any notes, is stored exclusively in your device's local storage (browser storage). This means:
privacy.section2Item1: No servers. Your data is never uploaded to any server, cloud service, or database operated by IPC or any third party.
privacy.section2Item2: No transmission. Your data never leaves your device unless you explicitly choose to export it (see Section 4).
privacy.section2Item3: No remote access. IPC employees, contractors, or affiliates cannot access, view, or retrieve your data at any time.
privacy.section2Item4: Device-only. If you clear your browser data or uninstall the App, your data will be permanently deleted and cannot be recovered by us.
privacy.section3Text: The App allows you to record information related to your bladder and fluid intake patterns. This information may be considered health-related data. We want to be clear:
privacy.section3Item1: We never collect, process, store, or have access to your health information.
privacy.section3Item2: Your health data remains solely on your device under your control.
privacy.section3Item3: We do not sell, share, license, or disclose any user data to any third party, because we do not have any user data.
privacy.section4Text: The App provides an optional export feature that allows you to download your diary data as a PDF or CSV file. When you use this feature:
privacy.section4Item1: The file is generated entirely on your device.
privacy.section4Item2: The file is saved to your device's local downloads folder.
privacy.section4Item3: Only you decide whether, when, and with whom to share the exported file (for example, with your health professional).
privacy.section4Item4: IPC has no knowledge of whether you export your data or who you share it with.
privacy.section5Item5: Fingerprinting or any user identification technology
privacy.section5Footer: The App uses browser local storage solely to save your diary entries on your device. This is not a cookie and is not accessible to any website or service other than the App itself.
privacy.section6Text: If you grant notification permission, the App may send you local reminders to help you remember to log your entries. These notifications are:
privacy.section6Item2: Not sent through any external server or push notification service
privacy.section6Item3: Optional. You can decline or revoke permission at any time through your device settings.
privacy.section7Text: The App is intended for adults and is designed to be used under the guidance of a health professional. We do not knowingly collect information from children under the age of 13. Since we do not collect any personal information from any user, this policy applies equally to all age groups.
privacy.section8Text: The App does not integrate with or transmit data to any third-party services. The App does not contain any third-party SDKs that collect user data. The App may load fonts from Google Fonts for display purposes; no personal data is transmitted in this process.
privacy.section9Text: Since all data is stored locally on your device:
privacy.section9Item1: You control retention. Your data exists for as long as you keep it on your device.
privacy.section9Item2: You control deletion. You can delete all data at any time by tapping "Start a New Tracking" in the App, clearing your browser data, or uninstalling the App.
privacy.section9Item3: We cannot retain your data because we never receive it.
privacy.section10Text: Because your data never leaves your device, the primary security responsibility rests with your device's own security measures (screen lock, device encryption, etc.). We recommend:
privacy.section10Item1: Keeping your device's operating system and browser up to date
privacy.section10Item2: Using a screen lock (PIN, fingerprint, or face recognition)
privacy.section10Item3: Not sharing your device with others if you have private health data in the App
privacy.section11Text: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Any changes will be reflected in the "Last updated" date at the top of this page. Since we do not collect contact information, we cannot directly notify you of changes. We encourage you to review this policy periodically.
privacy.section12Text: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have rights regarding your personal data, including rights of access, correction, deletion, and portability. Since we do not collect or store any personal data, these rights are inherently fulfilled. You have complete control over all your data at all times, directly on your device.
privacy.section13Text: If you have any questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact:
privacy.copyright: © {year} Integrated Pelvic Care Inc. All rights reserved.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.metaDescription: Terms of Use for My Flow Check. This app is for personal tracking only and does not provide medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment.
terms.intro: Welcome to My Flow Check (the "App"), operated by Integrated Pelvic Care Inc. ("IPC", "we", "us", or "our"). By accessing or using the App, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Use ("Terms"). If you do not agree to these Terms, please do not use the App.
terms.section1Text: By downloading, installing, accessing, or using the App, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms and our <privacyLink>Privacy Policy</privacyLink>. These Terms constitute a legally binding agreement between you and IPC. We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time, and your continued use of the App after such modifications constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms.
terms.section2Text: My Flow Check is a personal tracking tool that allows users to record fluid intake, bathroom visits, bedtimes, and wake times over a 3-day period. The App generates a summary of your recorded data that you may optionally share with a health professional. The App is designed as a supplementary tool to support conversations with your healthcare provider.
terms.section3Disclaimer: My Flow Check is NOT a medical device, diagnostic tool, or treatment platform. It does not provide medical advice, diagnosis, prognosis, or treatment recommendations of any kind.
terms.section3Text: The App is intended solely for personal informational and educational tracking purposes. Specifically:
terms.section3Item1: The App does not diagnose any medical condition, disease, or disorder.
terms.section3Item2: The App does not recommend, suggest, or imply any course of treatment, medication, or therapy.
terms.section3Item3: The App does not replace professional medical advice, examination, or diagnosis.
terms.section3Item4: The App does not interpret your data or draw medical conclusions from your entries.
terms.section3Item5: No information presented in the App should be construed as medical advice or a substitute for professional consultation.
terms.section3Warning: Always seek the advice of a qualified health professional with any questions you may have regarding a medical condition. Never disregard professional medical advice or delay seeking it because of information you have recorded in or obtained from this App.
terms.section4Text: The App is intended for use by adults (18 years of age or older) and is designed to be used under the guidance of a health professional. By using the App, you represent and warrant that you are at least 18 years of age. If you are under 18, you may only use the App under the supervision of a parent, guardian, or healthcare provider.
terms.section5Text: When using the App, you agree to:
terms.section5Item1: Use the App only for its intended purpose of personal health tracking.
terms.section5Item2: Provide accurate information to the best of your ability when logging entries.
terms.section5Item3: Not rely on the App as a substitute for professional medical care, advice, or diagnosis.
terms.section5Item4: Consult a qualified health professional before making any health-related decisions based on data recorded in the App.
terms.section5Item5: Maintain the security of your device, as all data is stored locally on your device.
terms.section5Item6: Not use the App for any unlawful, harmful, or unauthorized purpose.
terms.section5Item7: Not attempt to reverse-engineer, decompile, disassemble, or otherwise attempt to derive the source code of the App.
terms.section5Item8: Not reproduce, distribute, modify, create derivative works of, publicly display, or publicly perform any part of the App without prior written consent from IPC.
terms.section6Text: All data you enter into the App is stored exclusively on your device. IPC does not collect, store, access, or transmit any of your personal or health data. You retain full ownership and control over your data at all times. For complete details, please review our <privacyLink>Privacy Policy</privacyLink>.
terms.section6Item1: If you clear your browser data, uninstall the App, or reset your device, your data will be permanently lost and cannot be recovered by IPC.
terms.section6Item2: IPC is not responsible for any data loss resulting from device malfunction, browser updates, cache clearing, or any other cause.
terms.section6Item3: You are solely responsible for exporting and backing up your data if you wish to preserve it.
terms.section6Item4: When you choose to export and share your data (e.g., with a health professional), you do so at your own discretion and risk.
terms.section7Text: The App provides an optional export feature that allows you to download your diary data as a file. You acknowledge that:
terms.section7Item1: Any exported data is generated entirely on your device.
terms.section7Item2: You are solely responsible for how you store, share, and distribute exported data.
terms.section7Item3: IPC has no control over, and accepts no responsibility for, how you use or share your exported data.
terms.section7Item4: Once exported, your data is subject to the security measures (or lack thereof) of the platforms or methods you choose to use for storage or transmission.
terms.section8Text: The App, including but not limited to its design, text, graphics, logos, icons, images, software, code, and overall appearance ("Content"), is the property of Integrated Pelvic Care Inc. and is protected by Canadian and international copyright, trademark, and other intellectual property laws.
terms.section8Item1: The "My Flow Check" name and logo are trademarks of IPC.
terms.section8Item2: You are granted a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable license to use the App for personal, non-commercial purposes in accordance with these Terms.
terms.section8Item3: This license does not include the right to modify, distribute, sell, lease, or create derivative works based on the App or its Content.
terms.section9Disclaimer1: THE APP IS PROVIDED ON AN "AS IS" AND "AS AVAILABLE" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, EITHER EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, ACCURACY, COMPLETENESS, NON-INFRINGEMENT, OR AVAILABILITY.
terms.section9Disclaimer2: IPC DOES NOT WARRANT THAT THE APP WILL BE UNINTERRUPTED, ERROR-FREE, SECURE, OR FREE OF VIRUSES OR OTHER HARMFUL COMPONENTS. IPC DOES NOT WARRANT THE ACCURACY, RELIABILITY, OR COMPLETENESS OF ANY DATA OR INFORMATION DISPLAYED, GENERATED, OR PROCESSED BY THE APP.
terms.section9Disclaimer3: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, IN NO EVENT SHALL IPC, ITS DIRECTORS, OFFICERS, EMPLOYEES, AGENTS, CONTRACTORS, AFFILIATES, OR LICENSORS BE LIABLE FOR ANY DIRECT, INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, EXEMPLARY, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH YOUR USE OF OR INABILITY TO USE THE APP, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO:
terms.section9DamageItem1: Any health decisions made based on data recorded in the App
terms.section9DamageItem2: Any delay in seeking medical treatment
terms.section9DamageItem3: Any misinterpretation of data recorded in the App
terms.section9DamageItem4: Loss of data stored on your device
terms.section9DamageItem6: Any reliance on the App for health-related decision-making
terms.section9DamageItem7: Device malfunction, browser incompatibility, or software conflicts
terms.section9Disclaimer4: THIS LIMITATION OF LIABILITY APPLIES WHETHER THE ALLEGED LIABILITY IS BASED ON CONTRACT, TORT (INCLUDING NEGLIGENCE), STRICT LIABILITY, OR ANY OTHER BASIS, EVEN IF IPC HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGE.
terms.section10Text: You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless IPC, its directors, officers, employees, agents, contractors, and affiliates from and against any and all claims, liabilities, damages, losses, costs, and expenses (including reasonable legal fees) arising out of or in connection with:
terms.section10Item3: Your violation of any applicable law or regulation
terms.section10Item4: Any health decisions made based on data recorded in the App
terms.section10Item5: Your sharing of exported data with third parties
terms.section10Item6: Any claim that your use of the App caused damage to a third party
terms.section11Text: You expressly acknowledge and agree that your use of the App is at your sole risk. You understand that:
terms.section11Item1: The App is a personal tracking tool and is not a substitute for professional medical evaluation, advice, diagnosis, or treatment.
terms.section11Item2: Health conditions require professional medical assessment, and the App cannot and does not provide such assessment.
terms.section11Item3: Relying solely on data recorded in the App for health decisions could result in harm.
terms.section11Item4: The accuracy of the data in the App depends entirely on the information you enter, and the App does not verify or validate any entries.
terms.section11Item5: Local device storage may be subject to data loss from device failure, software updates, or user actions.
terms.section12Text: The App may offer optional local notification reminders to help you log entries. You acknowledge that:
terms.section12Item1: Notifications are provided as a convenience only and are not guaranteed to be delivered.
terms.section12Item2: IPC is not liable for missed notifications, delayed reminders, or any consequences arising from notification failures.
terms.section12Item3: You are solely responsible for logging your entries regardless of whether you receive notifications.
terms.section12Item4: Notification availability depends on your device settings, operating system, and browser support.
terms.section13Text: The App may contain links to third-party websites or services that are not owned or controlled by IPC. IPC has no control over, and assumes no responsibility for, the content, privacy policies, or practices of any third-party websites or services. You acknowledge and agree that IPC shall not be responsible or liable, directly or indirectly, for any damage or loss caused or alleged to be caused by or in connection with use of or reliance on any such content, goods, or services available on or through any such websites or services.
terms.section14Text: You may stop using the App at any time. IPC reserves the right to discontinue or modify the App at any time without notice. Since all data is stored locally on your device, discontinuation of the App will not affect data already stored on your device, but you will no longer receive updates or support.
terms.section15Text: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Province of Ontario and the federal laws of Canada applicable therein, without regard to conflict of law principles. Any dispute arising out of or in connection with these Terms or your use of the App shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts located in the Province of Ontario, Canada.
terms.section16Text: If any provision of these Terms is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable by a court of competent jurisdiction, such provision shall be modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid, legal, and enforceable. If such modification is not possible, the provision shall be severed from these Terms. The invalidity, illegality, or unenforceability of any provision shall not affect the validity or enforceability of the remaining provisions.
terms.section17Text: These Terms, together with the <privacyLink>Privacy Policy</privacyLink>, constitute the entire agreement between you and IPC regarding your use of the App and supersede all prior or contemporaneous agreements, understandings, representations, and warranties, whether written or oral, regarding the App.

hub.title: Understanding your body
hub.description: We all have a body. Sharing what we know about bladder health, urinary symptoms, and what to do about them, so we can all understand our own a bit better.
hub.noArticles: Articles coming soon. Check back shortly.

audience.menDescription: Articles relevant to men's bladder, prostate, and pelvic health.
audience.womenDescription: Articles for women's urinary and pelvic health, with referral to specialized resources.

forMen.title: Men's bladder and pelvic health
forMen.description: Articles on men's bladder, prostate, and pelvic health. Track your symptoms with our 3-day diary.
forMen.intro: Men experience bladder and pelvic symptoms across the lifespan. Most are treatable once understood. The articles below cover the most common patterns: waking up at night, urgency, weak stream, prostate-related changes. If you want to track your own pattern, use the 3-day bladder diary.
forMen.ctaDescription: Our bladder diary is built specifically for men's pelvic health, following the IPC methodology used by clinicians.
forMen.noArticles: No articles for men yet. Check back soon.

forWomen.title: Women's pelvic and urinary health
forWomen.description: Articles on women's urinary and pelvic health.
forWomen.intro: Women's urinary and pelvic health spans many conditions: stress incontinence, urgency, recurrent UTIs, pelvic organ prolapse, and the changes that come with pregnancy and menopause. The articles below offer plain-language explanations and practical guidance.
forWomen.ctaTitle: Looking for a women's pelvic health specialist?
forWomen.ctaDescription: Our 3-day diary is built for men's pelvic health. For women, we recommend consulting a clinician specialized in female pelvic care or a pelvic floor physiotherapist.
forWomen.noArticles: No articles for women yet. Check back soon.

topic.noArticles: No articles in this topic yet.

article.ctaForMen: Track your symptoms with our 3-day bladder diary
article.ctaForWomen: Find a women's pelvic health specialist

glossary.title: Glossary
glossary.description: Definitions of medical terms used in our articles.

learn.disclaimer: This article is for educational purposes only. It does not provide medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Always consult a qualified health professional regarding any medical condition.
